folio.label: 121v
folio.l: It failletú not , whil it is in ©oure wolde

note.notetxt: 159there is a cross in dry point next to the lines.ODR

folio.l: ¢ And whan this knygút , hatú thus his tale I tolde
He ridetú out of halle , and doun he ligút
His Steede whicú that shone , as sonne brigút
Stant in the courte , as stille as any ston
This knygút is to his chaumbre , lad a non
And is vnarmed , and to mete I sette
The presentes , ben ful richelicú I fette
This is to seyn , the swerd and the Mirour
And born a non , vn to the heigú Tour
Witú certeyne officers , ordeyned therfore
And to Canacee , the Ryngö is bore
Solempnely , there she sat at the table
But sekerly , witú outen any fable
The hors of Bras , that can nougút be remewed
It stant as it were , to the ground I glewed
There may noman , out of that place it dryue
For non engyne , of wyndas or polyue
And cause why , for they co~ne nougút the craft
And therfore in the place , they han it laft
Til that the knygút , hatú taugút hem the manere
To voiden hi~ , as ©e shuln after here
¢ Greet was the prees , that swarmetú to and froo
To gauren on this hors , that stondetú so
For it so heigú was , & so brode and longe
So wel proporcioned , for to be stronge
Rigút as it were a Steede , of lumbardie
There witú so horsly , and so quyk at eye
As it a gentil poleis , courser were
For certes from his taille , vn to his ere
Nature ne art , ne coude hi~ nougút amende
In no degree , as al the peple wende
But euäe moo , here moost wondre was
How that it coude gon , & was of bras
It was a fayrie , as the peeple semed
Diuäse folk , diuäsely han demed
As many hedes , as many wyttes ben
They mormered , as dotú a swarm of been
And maden Skyles , after here fantasies
Rehersyngö , of this olde poetries
And seiden it was like , the pegasee
The hors that hadde , wenges for to flee
Or ellis it was , the Grekes hors Sinou~
That brougút Troye , to destrucciouö
As men moun , in theise olde gestes rede
